name: Czech Bread and Cheese

ingredients.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps: Butter the bread and top with the cheese.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps_detailed: Butter the bread generously, top with the cheese, and finish with a little black pepper.

name: Czech Oat Porridge
steps: Simmer the oats in the milk for 6-8 minutes, stirring, until thick.
Top with the honey.
steps_detailed: Simmer the oats in the milk and cinnamon over medium-low heat, stirring often, 8-10 minutes, until thick and creamy.
Top generously with the honey.

name: Czech Eggs with Peppers
steps: Soften the pepper and onion in the butter, 5-6 minutes.
Scramble in the eggs, cooking gently until just set.
steps_detailed: Soften the pepper and onion in the butter over medium heat, 6-7 minutes.
Scramble in the eggs and black pepper, cooking gently, stirring often, until just set.

name: Czech Veprove Knedlo Zelo (Roast Pork, Dumplings, Sauerkraut)
steps: Season the pork with the caraway and roast at 400F for 25-28 minutes until browned.
Simmer the sauerkraut in a little water alongside.
Mix the flour with water and a pinch of salt, shape into a small dumpling, and simmer 15-18 minutes until cooked through, then slice.
steps_detailed: Season the pork with the caraway and roast at 400F (200C) for 26-30 minutes until well browned and rendered.
Simmer the sauerkraut in a little water and the pork drippings, 10-12 minutes.
Mix the flour, egg, butter, and enough water into a firm dough, shape into a small loaf, and simmer 18-20 minutes until cooked through, then slice.
Veprove knedlo zelo - roast pork, bread dumpling, and sauerkraut together on one plate - is considered the Czech national dish.

name: Czech Goulash

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Brown the beef with the onion and paprika.
Add 1.5 cups water and simmer 30-35 minutes until tender.
Mix the flour with water into a firm dough, shape into a small dumpling, and simmer 15 minutes.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, garlic, and paprika over medium-high heat, 9-11 minutes.
Add 1.75 cups water and simmer 32-38 minutes until fully tender.
Mix the flour, egg, and enough water into a firm dough, shape into a dumpling, and simmer 16-18 minutes until cooked through, then slice and serve alongside.
Czech gulas is generally thicker and served with bread dumplings, distinct from the soupier Hungarian original it borrows its name from.

name: Czech Potato Dumplings
steps: Mix the mashed potato with the flour and egg into a firm dough.
Shape into balls and simmer in water 10-12 minutes until they float.
Toss in the melted butter.
steps_detailed: Mix the mashed potato with the flour, egg, and pepper into a firm, workable dough.
Shape into even balls and simmer in gently boiling water 12-14 minutes until they float and are cooked through.
Toss in the melted butter before serving.

name: Czech Fried Cheese (Smazeny Syr)
steps: Dredge the cheese in the flour, then the beaten egg.
Fry in the oil, 1-2 minutes per side, until the coating is golden and the cheese is warm and soft inside.
steps_detailed: Dredge the cheese in the flour with the pepper, then the beaten egg, pressing to coat well.
Fry in the oil over medium-high heat, 2-3 minutes per side, until the coating is deeply golden and the cheese is warm and just beginning to soften inside.
Smazeny syr, a thick slab of fried cheese usually served in a bread roll, is one of the most popular fast foods in the Czech Republic.

name: Czech Svickova (Beef in Cream Sauce)

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Brown the beef with the onion.
Add water and simmer until tender, 40 minutes.
Stir in the sour cream and serve with boiled potato.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the carrot, celeriac, and onion, 9-11 minutes.
Add 1.5 cups water, cover, and simmer 40-45 minutes until the beef is very tender and the vegetables have fully softened.

Lift out the beef, then blend the cooking liquid and vegetables together with the flour until completely smooth - this pureed root vegetable base, not a simple cream sauce, is what actually gives svickova its body and its name (from the way of slicing the beef, but the sauce is the real technical heart of the dish).
Return the smooth sauce to the pot, whisk in the sour cream, sugar, and lime juice off the heat, and warm through gently without boiling.
Slice the beef and serve under the sauce.

The root vegetable puree is what separates real svickova from a generic beef-in-cream-sauce dish - carrots and celeriac cooked down and blended into the sauce itself, not left as chunks, are the actual technique the dish is built on.

name: Czech Roast Pork with Caraway
steps: Season the pork with the caraway and pepper and roast at 400F for 25-30 minutes until browned.
Steam the cabbage alongside.
steps_detailed: Season the pork generously with the caraway, pepper, and garlic and roast at 400F (200C) for 28-32 minutes until deeply browned and the fat has rendered.
Steam the cabbage in the last 10-12 minutes and serve alongside.

name: Czech Beef and Pepper Stew

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Brown the beef with the onion and paprika.
Add the pepper and 1.5 cups water, and simmer 30-35 minutes until tender.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, garlic, and paprika over medium-high heat, 9-11 minutes.
Add the pepper and 1.75 cups water, and simmer 32-38 minutes until fully tender.

name: Czech Cheese and Potato Bake
steps: Layer the potato with the milk in a small dish and bake at 375F, covered, for 30 minutes.
Uncover, top with the cheese, and bake 10-15 more minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Layer the potato with the milk, butter, and pepper in a small baking dish, cover, and bake at 375F (190C) for 32-35 minutes until tender.
Uncover, top with the cheese, and bake 12-15 more minutes until golden and bubbling.

name: Czech Bread Dumpling Snack
steps: Mix the flour, egg, and pepper into a firm dough.
Shape into a small dumpling and simmer in water 12-15 minutes until cooked through.
Toss with the butter.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, egg, pepper, and softened onion into a firm, workable dough.
Shape into a small dumpling and simmer in gently boiling water 14-16 minutes until cooked through.
Slice and toss with the butter - simple bread dumpling is eaten on its own just as often as alongside a main course.

name: Czech Caraway Crackers
steps: Rub the butter into the flour and caraway.
Add the milk to form a firm dough, roll thin, cut into shapes, and bake at 375F for 12-15 minutes until crisp.
steps_detailed: Rub the butter into the flour, caraway, and pepper until crumbly.
Add the milk to form a firm dough, roll thin, cut into small shapes, and bake at 375F (190C) for 13-16 minutes until crisp and lightly golden.

name: Czech Cheese and Pepper Snack
steps: Slice the pepper into strips.
Serve with the cheese and a light spread of butter.
steps_detailed: Slice the pepper into strips and season lightly with black pepper.
Serve with the cheese and a light spread of butter - a simple, common snack plate.

name: Czech Pickled Sauerkraut Snack
steps: Toss the sauerkraut with the onion and oil.
steps_detailed: Toss the sauerkraut with the onion, oil, and caraway.
Let sit 10 minutes so the flavors meld before serving - a simple, sharp little side eaten alongside heavier dishes.

name: Czech Apricot Dumplings
steps: Mix the mashed potato with the flour into a soft dough.
Wrap portions of dough around each apricot, sealing well, and simmer in water 10-12 minutes until they float.
Roll in the sugar before serving.
steps_detailed: Mix the mashed potato with the flour into a soft, workable dough.
Wrap portions of dough around each pitted apricot, sealing well, and simmer in gently boiling water 12-14 minutes until they float.
Roll in the melted butter and sugar before serving - meruntkove knedliky, fruit-filled potato dumplings, appear with plums, apricots, or strawberries depending on the season.

name: Czech Almond Cake
steps: Cream the butter, beat in the egg, then fold in the flour and almonds.
Bake at 350F for 20-22 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
steps_detailed: Cream the butter and sugar until light, then beat in the egg.
Fold in the flour and almonds, pour into a small lined pan, and bake at 350F (175C) for 22-26 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.

name: Czech Rice Pudding
steps: Simmer the rice in the milk with the cinnamon and honey for 25-30 minutes until thick and creamy.
steps_detailed: Simmer the rice in the milk with the cinnamon, honey, and butter over low heat, stirring often, 28-32 minutes, until thick and creamy.

name: Czech Sour Cream Dill Sauce
steps: Warm the sour cream, dill, and butter together gently until smooth.
steps_detailed: Warm the sour cream, dill, butter, and pepper together gently over low heat, stirring, until smooth - do not let it boil.
Good over potatoes, fish, or dumplings.

name: Czech Caraway Onion Sauce
steps: Cook the onion slowly in the butter with the caraway until deeply softened, about 12-15 minutes.
steps_detailed: Cook the onion slowly in the butter with the caraway and pepper over medium-low heat, 15-18 minutes, until deeply softened and golden.
Good over roasted pork or dumplings.
